sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The batch executed 12 actions. Let me check where the green ended up. The animation_changed_bbox is [63, 45, 63, 52] which is the HUD timer area. But the board did change. Let me check the current state to see where the green is.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
for r in range(25, 35):
    row = lines[r]
    special = [(c, ch) for c, ch in enumerate(row) if ch in 'NBWc']
    if special:
        print(f"Row {r}: {special}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The green with W is still at rows 30-32, cols 30-32 (center at 31, 31). The moves didn't work as expected. The green didn't move at all!
Wait, that can't be right. Let me look more carefully. The green is still at rows 30-32, cols 30-32 with W at center. The left frame green at rows 33-35, cols 12-14 still has charcoal center.
But the animation says the board changed. Let me check if maybe the green moved and then came back, or if only the HUD changed.
Actually wait, looking at the ascii output more carefully, I don't see the green anywhere else. The green with W is at rows 30-32, cols 30-32. The right frame at rows 26-30, cols 44-48 is empty (just the charcoal border).
Hmm, so the 12 moves didn't actually move the green? That's strange. Let me check if maybe the green is blocked by something. The green is at rows 30-32, cols 30-32. To move UP, it would need to go to rows 27-29. Let me check if there's something blocking.
Actually, wait. Looking at the ascii, row 30 shows the green at cols 30-32. Row 27-29 show the right frame's border at cols 44-48. There's nothing at rows 27-29, cols 30-32 that would block the green.
But the green didn't move. Let me check if the moves are actually working by trying a single UP move and seeing what happens.
Actually, maybe the issue is that the green CAN'T move because it's not the "active" player piece. Let me reconsider. Earlier, I was able to move the green with W around. But maybe after clicking on the middle green, the "active" green changed?
Wait no, the middle green IS the green with W. It has W at center (31, 31). So it should be movable.
Let me try a single UP move to see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try a single UP move to see if the green moves
action(['UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
